---
description: "Use when working on frontend code in the Web project: React components, routes, TanStack Query hooks, TanStack Router, TanStack Table, Tailwind CSS styling, or Zod validation schemas. Covers UI conventions for the PlateLib plate discovery interface."
applyTo: "Web/**"
---
# Frontend Guidelines

## Stack

| Tool | Role |
|------|------|
| React 19 + TypeScript | UI framework |
| Vite | Build tool and dev server (port 3000) |
| TanStack Router | File-based routing with type-safe routes |
| TanStack Query | Server state management, data fetching |
| TanStack Table | Plate listing tables with sorting/filtering |
| Tailwind CSS v4 | Utility-first styling |
| Radix UI + shadcn/ui | Accessible component primitives |
| Zod | Schema validation for forms and API responses |
| pnpm | Package manager |

## Project Structure

```
Web/src/
├── components/         # Shared UI components (shadcn/ui pattern)
│   └── ui/             # Base Radix/shadcn primitives
├── routes/             # TanStack Router file-based routes
├── hooks/              # Custom React hooks
├── integrations/
│   └── tanstack-query/ # Query client setup, devtools provider
├── data/               # Static data or local constants
└── lib/                # Utility functions (cn, etc.)
```

## Routing

Use TanStack Router file-based routing. Routes live in `src/routes/`. The generated route tree is in `src/routeTree.gen.ts` — **do not edit this file manually**; it is auto-generated by the Vite plugin.

## Data Fetching

- Use TanStack Query (`useQuery`, `useMutation`) for all API calls.
- Define query keys and fetcher functions in `src/integrations/tanstack-query/` or co-locate with the feature they serve.
- Use Zod to validate API responses where type safety matters beyond TypeScript inference.
- Never fetch data directly inside route components without going through TanStack Query.

## Components

- Follow shadcn/ui conventions: components in `src/components/ui/` are low-level primitives; feature components compose them.
- Use `cn()` (from `src/lib/`) to conditionally merge Tailwind classes.
- Prefer Radix UI primitives for accessible interactive elements (Select, Switch, Slider, Label).
- Use `lucide-react` for icons.

## Plate Display

The core UI concern is helping users discover and compare well plates:
- Plate listings should show: name, manufacturer, well count, material, color, skirt, sterility, and available files.
- Plate files (PDFs, drawings) should be downloadable; show file name and type, never embed binary content in list queries.
- Filtering and sorting on plate properties should use TanStack Table's built-in capabilities with `@tanstack/match-sorter-utils` for fuzzy search.

## Forms & Validation

- Use TanStack Form (`@tanstack/react-form`) for all forms.
- Define Zod schemas for form validation and use them with TanStack Form's Zod adapter.

## Styling

- Use Tailwind CSS v4 utility classes; avoid inline styles.
- Component variants should use `class-variance-authority` (CVA).
- Use `tailwind-merge` (via `cn()`) when combining conditional class strings.

## Commands

```bash
pnpm dev      # Start dev server on port 3000
pnpm build    # Build for production
pnpm test     # Run Vitest tests
```
